retrieved_docs.title: Dental Caries -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: The International Caries Detection and Assessment System (ICDAS) is a useful tool for detecting and recording caries. This system classifies carious lesions into six categories, where a higher score correlates with a more advanced lesion. The ICDAS tracks the progression of carious lesions and is an accurate and reproducible caries classification system. [18] (Table 1).

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Caries Diagnostic Testing -- Procedures
retrieved_docs.content: The International Caries Detection and Assessment System (ICDAS) serves as a valuable tool in detecting and recording caries. The ICDAS classifies carious lesions into 6 categories, ranging from 0 to 6, where a higher score correlates with a more extensive lesion. The ICDAS can track the changes and progression of carious lesions. It is a specific, accurate, and reproducible classification system. [5] See Table. International Caries Detection and Assessment System Caries Score Table. Radiographic Diagnosis

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Caries Classification Systems -- Issues of Concern -- International Caries Detection and Assessment System
retrieved_docs.content: The ICDAS was developed to include the current knowledge about the pathogenesis of caries development, detecting carious lesions at an early stage, and quantifying the degree of demineralization. The ICDAS features an individualized scoring system to detect and assess primary coronal and root caries and caries associated with restorations and sealants (CARS). Using the ICDAS for root caries is yet to be implemented in epidemiological studies. [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Caries Diagnostic Testing -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Newer diagnostic tools, such as laser fluorescence detection devices and light-induced fluorescence, give more accurate information about the carious lesion. Light-induced fluorescence detects the lesion's specific location and extension, as well as bacterial activity, providing quantitative data. [1] Laser fluorescence detection devices detect remineralization and demineralization processes, which can be implemented to diagnose an incipient lesion and assess the efficacy of a remineralization therapy. Since this tool provides a numerical value, it is considered a quantitative test. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Caries Diagnostic Testing -- Procedures
retrieved_docs.content: PTR-LUM monitors caries-related changes in the tooth's microstructure and can be used adjunctively with radiographs. The measured changes correspond with optical and thermal properties measured by photodetectors and infrared detectors. The resulting PTR-LUM response is viewed on the Canary scale as a number between 1 and 100. A reading between 1 and 20 indicates that the mineralization corresponds with a healthy tooth. A number between 20 and 70 is indicative of demineralization. Lastly, a reading of 70 or greater is a strong indicator of advanced demineralization and decay and requires invasive treatment. [16] Depending on the number, the dental practitioner can determine whether invasive or noninvasive treatment is necessary. Laser Fluorescence Caries Detection Devices

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Caries Diagnostic Testing -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: The dentistry field is progressing towards a more conservative approach when treating dental caries. Newer diagnostic technology can detect initial stages of demineralization, allowing non-invasive intervention as early as possible to prevent further damage; this has invaluable repercussions on the oral health of the individual and broader community. However, more traditional methods, such as fiber optic transillumination, should not be disregarded since sufficient evidence supports their high specificity and sensitivity in caries diagnosis.

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Caries Diagnostic Testing -- Procedures
retrieved_docs.content: Dental explorers have traditionally diagnosed carious lesions; however, studies have shown that this method causes irreversible harm to the tooth surface and favors the lesion's progression. [3] Dental explorers can disrupt remineralization and transfer cariogenic bacteria to adjacent teeth. Furthermore, using dental explorers to assess occlusal surfaces can result in misdiagnosis - a deep fissure that seems to be a "sticky occlusal surface" could be misdiagnosed as dental caries. Dental explorers should only be used to remove stains and soft plaque from tooth surfaces. [4] Visual examination always comes first when assessing for caries. It requires a dry surface and can be enhanced by magnifying loupes.

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Caries Diagnostic Testing -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Dental caries remains one of the most widespread chronic infections globally. Fortunately, numerous diagnostic tests developed over the past century now enable earlier detection of the disease, supporting timely intervention and more effective management to address caries before significant progression occurs.

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Caries -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: The management of dental caries should be aimed at 1) detecting initial lesions, 2) determining caries activity, 3) performing a caries risk assessment, 4) preventing new carious lesions, 5) preserving dental tissue, and 6) maintaining teeth for as long as possible.

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Caries Diagnostic Testing -- Procedures
retrieved_docs.content: To assess proximal caries of anterior teeth, the provider should set the probe on the vestibulo-cervical aspect of the tooth and examine the tooth from the lingual face with a mouth mirror. To assess proximal caries of posterior teeth, the provider must set the probe on the cervical region of the tooth, either buccally or lingually. A flexible and thin fiber-optic tip has been recently developed to evaluate interproximal caries in posterior teeth, which must be glided below the proximal contact. [13] Latest Technologies to Evaluate Dental Caries

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Caries Classification Systems -- Issues of Concern -- International Caries Detection and Assessment System
retrieved_docs.content: The  detection arm of the ICDAS determines the features of the caries lesion. These features include the location of the lesion, whether it is coronal or radicular, and its restorative status. [4] The  assessment arm stages the lesion as cavitated or noncavitated, and active or inactive. [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Child Abuse and Neglect (Nursing) -- Assessment -- Neglect
retrieved_docs.content: Physical examination may not only demonstrate signs of physical abuse but may show signs of neglect. The general examination may show poor oral hygiene with extensive dental caries, malnutrition with significant growth failure, untreated diaper dermatitis, or untreated wounds.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 72
retrieved_docs.content: The category “diseases of mouth; excluding dental” is classified as a subcategory of “اِضطِراب الغدة اللُّعابِيَّة” (salivary gland disorder), indicating that non‑dental oral diseases are considered part of the broader spectrum of salivary gland pathology. This relationship highlights the importance of evaluating salivary gland function when assessing oral conditions that are not of dental origin, thereby guiding more comprehensive diagnosis and patient care.

retrieved_docs.content: diabetes mellitus, its specific complications (various forms of retinopathy and nephropathy), and the relationship between these complications and ocular destructive procedures is essential for comprehensive patient assessment and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 2 -- Community 895
retrieved_docs.content: 0.0088 MG/MG Toothpaste. Across these preparations, sodium monofluorophosphate provides fluoride ions that inhibit demineralization and promote remineralization of tooth enamel, thereby reducing the incidence of dental caries.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 1 -- Community 170
retrieved_docs.content: adverse‑event reporting and clinical management. Recognizing sodium monofluorophosphate as a fluoride source guides oral‑health preventive strategies, while awareness of the SMQ classification aids clinicians in systematic identification and treatment of functional GI disturbances, underscoring the importance of these entities for patient care and product design.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 249
retrieved_docs.content: Dental procedures are performed directly on zuby mléčné (the primary or “milk” teeth), indicating that therapeutic dental interventions often target the primary dentition. Likewise, disorders of teeth and jaw are located in zuby mléčné, showing that many oral pathologies also affect these early‑life teeth. Together, these relationships emphasize that both clinical treatments and disease processes in pediatric dentistry are closely linked to the primary teeth, underscoring the importance of careful assessment and management of zuby mléčné in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1990
retrieved_docs.content: Caries prophylactic agents constitute a class of substances employed to prevent dental caries, and both fluorid cínatý and Olaflur are identified as members of this class.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2614
retrieved_docs.content: Disease condition determination, uncontrolled is described as a general clinical state that is interpreted by clinicians (is_interpreted_by) and is associated with diabetes mellitus with complications. This indicates that an uncontrolled disease condition determination reflects a concerning overall clinical status and frequently co‑occurs with complicated diabetes mellitus, highlighting the need for careful assessment and targeted management in patients presenting with such uncontrolled conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 250
retrieved_docs.content: The extraction of a single tooth is a direct dental procedure performed on the zuby (teeth), involving the removal of one tooth from the oral cavity.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 4 -- Community 2774
retrieved_docs.content: In medical terminology, the designation “Allowed qualifier” is applied within the scope of enzymology to the conditions قَرْحَة مَعِدِيّة (gastric ulcer), intestinal infection, and اِضطِراب اللسان (tongue disorder). This indicates that enzyme‑related criteria or measurements are permissible as qualifiers when evaluating these gastrointestinal and oral disorders, thereby supporting more precise diagnostic assessment and targeted patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 3 -- Community 2732
retrieved_docs.content: Accidental physical contact (event) is described as being due to loss of teeth caused by an accident, indicating that dental injury resulting from an accident can lead to unintended physical contact.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 244
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction of single tooth is classified as a dental procedure, indicating that it has a relationship with the broader category of dental procedures. This connection underscores that the act of extracting a single tooth is a specific type of dental intervention within the overall field of dental procedures.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2479
retrieved_docs.content: Disease condition determination, uncontrolled is associated with diabetes mellitus with complications, indicating that an uncontrolled disease state is linked to the presence of diabetes mellitus that has progressed to a complicated form, underscoring the importance of effective disease management to prevent or mitigate diabetic complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 3 -- Community 2387
retrieved_docs.content: The phrase “Due to” serves as a causal indicator and is associated with specific injury outcomes, including loss of teeth resulting from an accident (“verlies; tanden door ongeval”) as well as severe trauma such as crushing injuries or internal injuries. Recognizing this wording helps clinicians link the underlying cause to these particular clinical presentations.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 4 -- Community 2857
retrieved_docs.content: The term “Allowed qualifier” denotes that radiotherapy (“radioterapie”) is recognized as an acceptable therapeutic option for patients with tongue disorders (اِضطِراب اللسان), indicating that the use of radiotherapy is permitted and appropriate in the management of this specific oral condition.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 3 -- Community 1459
retrieved_docs.content: Microbiology is identified as an applicable qualifier for the oral conditions قيلة مثانية, قيلة مستقيمية, and اضطراب اللسان, indicating that microbiological factors are relevant in the assessment or management of these disorders. Bacterial vaccines are, in turn, an applicable qualifier for microbiology, reflecting the role of immunization strategies within the broader microbiological context. The term “Without” is noted as not being associated with either قيلة مثانية or قيلة مستقيمية, suggesting that the absence of a particular factor does not have a direct relationship with these specific conditions. Together, these connections highlight the importance of microbiological considerations and vaccine‑related interventions in certain oral pathologies, while clarifying that “Without” does not contribute to their etiology or presentation.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2813
retrieved_docs.content: Ultrasonography of head and neck is classified as a form of diagnostic ultrasound of the head and neck.
